description: §8.3 / §52.1 / §12.2 SQL hydrate round-trip (RUNTIME, serverDb driver) — a client button calls the server fn loadUsers (a bare `SELECT id, name FROM users`.all()); the E-ADAPTER server-eval path evaluates the REAL emitted route handler, whose `_scrml_sql` returns the seeded rows, and the client-bound cell @users hydrates the array. The <each> then renders one .row per row and #count (reading @users.length) reads 2. Pure DATA-FLOW: no WHERE / JOIN / ordering (the SQL stub returns the whole seeded table — the case seeds exactly the rows it asserts).
rationale: §12.2: loadUsers touches a server resource (`?{}` SQL) so it auto-escalates to server; the client call round-trips through the compiler-emitted route (serverDb server-eval runs the REAL handler). §8.3: `.all()` (≡ bare `?{}`) resolves to Row[] — all rows as an array — which hydrates the reactive cell @users; a settle-drained server-fn write re-renders the <each> rows AND the @users.length interpolation. The seeded rows reach the client verbatim and render; asserted purely as data-flow (no WHERE-filter / write-back / ordering — the BLOCKED engine-semantics surface). OQ1: whole-tree dom omitted — the async hydrate + <each> container are impl-private; the anchored .row count + #count text + @users state are impl-neutral.
